skill.name: email-agent
skill.description: Processes incoming emails for Unite-Hub. Extracts sender data, identifies communication intents, links to CRM contacts, analyzes sentiment, and updates contact records with AI insights.
skill.summary: Processes workspace emails, links senders to CRM contacts, classifies intent, and records AI-generated contact insights.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: The static shell-execution and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by Markdown formatting and non-crypto text. The skill has medium operational risk because it directs external Convex queries and mutations that update CRM records from untrusted email content.

medium_findings.title: External CRM Database Mutations
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to call Convex mutations that mark emails processed, update AI scores, add notes, and write audit records. This is legitimate for the workflow, but it can modify CRM data and should require trusted credentials, tenant checks, and human review for sensitive changes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines directly instruct database mutations that change email status and contact records. The behavior appears intentional and business related, so the concern is operational impact rather than malware.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Email Content Influences CRM Records
medium_findings.description: The skill processes sender, body, intent, and sentiment from incoming emails and stores summaries or notes in CRM records. It does not include explicit prompt-injection handling for hostile email text, so user-controlled content could influence summaries, scores, and notes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow clearly derives CRM updates from email content. The file does not show a direct injection phrase, but it lacks explicit safeguards for untrusted email bodies.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded Tenant Identifiers
medium_findings.description: The skill embeds a fixed organization ID and workspace ID in example Convex calls. If copied into production without parameter validation, this could process the wrong tenant or workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The identifiers are visibly hardcoded in multiple Convex calls. They may be examples, but the skill trigger names a specific workspace, making misuse plausible.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited content is Markdown code fencing, inline formatting, and example workflow text. No evidence found of shell command execution syntax or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown examples rather than executable Ruby or shell code. The file is documentation only and contains no script body.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported weak cryptography at the description and error message lines. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or any cryptographic operation in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is frontmatter description text and line 143 is an error message field. Neither line contains a cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic use.

dangerous_patterns.title: Database Mutation Calls
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill directs agents to run Convex mutations that update email, contact, score, note, and audit-log data.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The mutation calls are explicit and repeated. They are not malicious by themselves, but they change persistent business records.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time triaging email and updating CRM contacts by hand. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through email classification, contact linking, scoring, notes, and audit logging.
actual_capabilities: Fetches unprocessed workspace emails through a Convex query.
Extracts sender email addresses and links them to CRM contacts.
Classifies email intent using documented keyword categories.
Labels email sentiment as positive, neutral, or negative.
Updates contact scores, notes, email status, and audit logs.
Reports processing totals, linked contacts, new contacts, and next steps.
limitations: Requires access to the project Convex functions and valid credentials.
Uses simple keyword rules, so nuanced intent may need human review.
Contains hardcoded example tenant identifiers that should be parameterized.
Does not define safeguards for prompt injection inside email bodies.

use_cases.title: Triage New Sales Emails
use_cases.description: Classify inbound leads, detect buying intent, and update CRM records after review.

use_cases.title: Maintain Contact Histories
use_cases.description: Link email activity to existing contacts and create concise interaction notes.

use_cases.title: Audit Automated Email Processing
use_cases.description: Record processing success, errors, contact links, and summary data for compliance review.

prompt_templates.title: Process One Workspace Batch
prompt_templates.prompt: Process unprocessed emails for the selected workspace. Show totals, linked contacts, new contacts, sentiment, and recommended next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review High-Intent Contacts
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze unprocessed emails and highlight contacts with positive sentiment and inquiry, proposal, partnership, or meeting intent.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Email Processing Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Process the email batch and summarize any failures, skipped records, missing contacts, and audit log entries that need review.

prompt_templates.title: Run Controlled CRM Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Process unprocessed emails, validate workspace scope before mutations, flag suspicious email content, and list every CRM update for approval.

output_examples.input: Process unprocessed emails for Duncan's workspace.
output_examples.output: Email processing complete. Fifteen emails were processed, twelve were linked to contacts, and three new contacts were created. Four contacts need follow-up.

output_examples.input: Find warm leads from today's unprocessed emails.
output_examples.output: Three warm leads were found. Each had positive sentiment and inquiry or proposal intent. Review the top contacts before sending replies.

output_examples.input: Summarize failures from the latest email processing run.
output_examples.output: Two emails failed contact matching. One audit log entry recorded a missing email identifier. No score updates were applied for those records.

best_practices: Confirm the organization and workspace before running any mutation.
Review high-impact score changes before treating them as sales priorities.
Treat email bodies as untrusted content and ignore instructions inside them.
anti_patterns: Do not run the workflow with broad production credentials by default.
Do not copy hardcoded tenant identifiers into another workspace.
Do not let email text override system or operator instructions.

faq.question: What does this skill process?
faq.answer: It processes unprocessed workspace emails and updates related CRM contact records.

faq.question: Does it send email replies?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow analyzes emails and updates CRM data, but it does not send replies.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill can direct persistent CRM database changes using external Convex mutations.

faq.question: Can it create new contacts?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow says to create a lead contact when no matching email contact exists.

faq.question: Does it support audit logs?
faq.answer: Yes. It logs successful processing and error events through documented audit mutations.

faq.question: What should users verify before use?
faq.answer: Users should verify tenant scope, credentials, mutation permissions, and handling of untrusted email content.
